Service 5 80,402

bwpageid:: 80402::

An Act to provide for the supplementary withdrawal and expenditure of funds from the
Treasury Fund to meet the expenditure of the Republic for the financial year ending on
30 June 2026.
Enacted by the Parliament of Nauru as follows:
1 Short title
This Act may be cited as the Supplementary Appropriation
Act (No 3) 2025–2026.
2 Commencement and expiry
(1) This Act commences on the day it is certified by the Speaker.
(2) This Act shall cease to have effect on 30 June 2026.
3 Withdrawal of funds from the Treasury Fund
(1) Recognises an additional $750,000 in revenue for the specific purposes
identified in the fifth column of Schedule 2 as detailed in the
Supplementary Estimates of Expenditure presented to Parliament on
14 January 2026.
(2) Recognises an additional $750,000 expenditure for the specific purposes
identified in the fifth column of Schedule 2 as detailed in the
Supplementary Estimates of Expenditure presented to Parliament on
14 January 2026.
(3) An amount not exceeding $750,000 may be withdrawn from the Treasury
Fund and expended as authorised in the fifth column of Schedule 2 and
detailed in the Supplementary Estimates of Expenditure presented to
Parliament on 14 January 2026.
(4) The amounts in the respective Schedules are in addition to and not in
substitution for the amounts previously authorised for withdrawal under
the Appropriation Act 2025–2026.
(5) Where the Cabinet considers it necessary to increase the amount assigned
in the estimates to a particular subhead within a Head, the Cabinet may
direct that there shall be applied to the aid of that subhead a further sum out
of any surplus arising on any other subhead within the same Head, but so
that the total amount withdrawn and expended does not exceed the amount
authorised for that Head in the Schedule to this Act.
